terms.definition: Abbreviation for t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding; non-linear embedding method for visualization, introduced by van der Maaten and Hinton (2008).
Full name of t-SNE: matches high-D Gaussian neighbor probabilities to low-D Student-t probabilities via KL divergence.
Hyperparameter controlling the effective number of neighbors each point uses when building high-D affinities; related to the entropy of conditional neighbor distributions.
Pairwise similarity weight p_ij in input space or q_ij in embedding space; high affinity means the pair should remain close in the map.
In low dimensions, too many equidistant high-D neighbors cannot all be placed at moderate distance from a center point; Student-t heavy tails mitigate this in t-SNE.
Initial optimization phase that temporarily multiplies p_ij to encourage wider separation of clusters before fine-tuning local structure.
